title: Tooltip component for React interfaces
description: Tooltip component for React and Next.js—hover and focus hints for dense dashboards without cluttering the default layout.

twitter.title: Tooltip · Zentauri UI
twitter.description: Tooltip component for React and Next.js—hover and focus hints for dense dashboards without cluttering the default layout.

intro: Tooltips surface short explanations on hover or focus—icon buttons, truncated labels, and chart markers. Use them in React dashboards and Next.js admin tools with disciplined copy length.

faqs.question: Does the Tooltip component work with Next.js App Router?
faqs.answer: Yes. Import it like any other React component; keep interactive subtrees in client components when you need hooks or browser APIs, and leave static structure in server components where possible.

faqs.question: Can I customize tooltip with Tailwind CSS?
faqs.answer: Zentauri UI exposes class-friendly variants and slots so you can extend styles with Tailwind utilities without fighting inline styles.

faqs.question: Is this Tooltip implementation accessible by default?
faqs.answer: Primitives follow sensible defaults, but accessibility depends on how you label controls, manage focus, and wire keyboard handlers in your app. Validate critical flows with keyboard-only use and screen readers.

sections.heading: What it does
sections.body:
Delay appearance slightly to avoid tooltip storms while moving the pointer.

Ensure triggers remain focusable for keyboard users.

sections.heading: Composition and API
sections.body:
Prefer compound subcomponents instead of one oversized prop bag. Export a small, documented API for your design system.

Do not place essential instructions only inside tooltips.

sections.heading: Accessibility
sections.body:
Keyboard order, focus rings, and ARIA attributes should match production usage. Test with your supported browsers and assistive technologies when semantics are non-trivial.

This preview page exposes a single h1 in the hero for a clean outline.

sections.heading: Next.js integration notes
sections.body:
Colocate examples under the App Router, keep server and client boundaries explicit, and avoid pulling interactive overlays into unexpected server layouts.

Set NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL so canonical and Open Graph URLs resolve on deploy.
